blueprints.name: Workforce Analytics Dashboard (Foundation)
blueprints.description: Basic headcount, turnover, and diversity dashboard for HR leadership providing visibility into core workforce metrics with row-level security for sensitive reporting.

patternFlow.description: Gold-layer tables feed directly into DirectLake semantic model without data duplication
DirectLake semantic model powers interactive Power BI dashboards with instant query response
Power BI reports enforce row-level security to restrict data by HR role and business unit
RLS-protected fields labeled with sensitivity tags for audit compliance
Sensitive headcount and compensation data encrypted with customer-managed keys

blueprints.editableNotes: Customize this blueprint for your organization by selecting which workforce metrics (headcount by department, turnover trends, diversity breakdowns) are most relevant to your HR leadership KPIs. Adjust RLS roles based on your org hierarchy and reporting requirements.
keyGovernanceRequirements: Define RLS roles matching HR organizational structure
Classify headcount and diversity data with sensitivity labels
Encrypt Lakehouse storage with customer-managed keys
Establish refresh SLA for daily HR metrics

activities: Define key HR metrics and KPIs
Validate data accuracy and business logic
Design dashboard layouts and user experience

blueprints.businessCase: Reduces time-to-insight for core workforce metrics from weeks to minutes. Eliminates shadow analytics by providing a single source of truth for headcount, turnover, and diversity. Enables HR leaders to make data-driven decisions on headcount planning and resource allocation with real-time dashboards and row-level security.

blueprints.name: Employee Attrition Risk Prediction Pipeline
blueprints.description: ML-based flight risk scoring with fairness controls and bias evaluation to identify at-risk employees while ensuring equitable predictions across demographic groups.
blueprints.audience: Data Scientists, HR Analytics, Talent Retention Teams

patternFlow.description: Gold-layer employee and engagement data extracted for feature engineering
Engineered features (tenure, promotion history, engagement scores) stored in versioned feature tables
Features fed into batch scoring pipeline that predicts attrition probability monthly
Predictions evaluated for bias across age, gender, and tenure groups
Fair, bias-evaluated models registered with fairness metrics and thresholds
New models tested against champion production model before promotion
High-risk employee scores trigger alerts to HR managers via Data Activator actions

blueprints.editableNotes: Customize this blueprint by selecting features most predictive of attrition in your organization (e.g., manager stability, compensation benchmarking, internal promotion velocity). Define fairness thresholds and bias tolerance by demographic group based on HR policy.
keyGovernanceRequirements: Track fairness metrics and bias evaluation results in MLflow
Document feature selection and model training datasets for auditability
Establish human review process for high-risk employee scores before HR action
Maintain model retraining schedule and performance monitoring dashboard

activities: Feature store and Delta table infrastructure setup
Define attrition drivers and business context
Validate model predictions against HR intuition
Design escalation workflows for high-risk employees

blueprints.businessCase: Reduces attrition-related replacement costs (estimated 50-200% of salary per departure) by identifying flight risks 3-6 months before resignation. Fairness controls ensure equitable treatment across demographics, reducing legal exposure. Enables targeted retention programs with measurable ROI impact.

blueprints.name: Secure Compensation Analytics
blueprints.description: Salary and bonus analytics with dynamic masking, DLP policy enforcement, and executive-level data isolation to enable compensation analytics while protecting sensitive payroll information.
blueprints.audience: Compensation & Benefits Teams, Finance, Executive Leadership

patternFlow.description: Gold-layer compensation data maintains history using Slowly Changing Dimension Type 2 for salary changes
Compensation amounts masked based on user role (HR sees actuals, managers see bands, employees see anonymized)
RLS ensures compensation data visible only to appropriate stakeholders (compensation team, relevant managers)
DLP policies prevent export or copying of compensation data to unsecured locations
Aggregate compensation analytics anonymized with k-anonymity to prevent individual identification
Anonymized, aggregated compensation metrics certified for self-service use by HR analysts
Paginated executive compensation reports with audit trails and DLP watermarks

blueprints.editableNotes: Customize masking rules to match your organization's compensation governance (e.g., what executive levels see individual vs. aggregate data). Define RLS groups for compensation team, business unit managers, and executives. Adjust k-anonymity thresholds based on your workforce size.
keyGovernanceRequirements: Define dynamic masking rules for each user role (HR specialist, manager, employee, executive)
Establish DLP policies preventing compensation data export and enforcing watermarking
Document SCD Type 2 salary history changes with effective dates for audit compliance
Monitor and audit compensation data access through sensitivity labels and SIEM integration

activities: SCD Type 2 implementation for salary history
Semantic model development with masking rules
Review k-anonymity thresholds with compensation team
Executive report content curation and approval

blueprints.businessCase: Ensures transparent, equitable salary decisions while protecting sensitive compensation data. Enables compensation benchmarking against external markets, identifying pay equity gaps by gender and ethnicity. Supports salary planning with scenario analysis, reducing compensation-related legal risk and litigation costs.

blueprints.name: Regulatory Compliance & Audit Stack
blueprints.description: Full governance stack for OSFI and Federal Reserve regulated environments with encryption, sensitivity classification, audit trails, and privileged access management.
blueprints.audience: Compliance Officers, Internal Audit, Security Teams, Risk Management

patternFlow.description: All HR data encrypted with customer-managed keys, then classified by sensitivity level (regulatory, confidential, internal)
Sensitivity labels propagated through Purview to create data lineage map with classification metadata
Purview metadata triggers DLP policies restricting access and export of highly sensitive HR data
DLP policy violations and access attempts logged to SIEM for regulatory audit and forensics
SIEM audit data correlates with workspace-level permission changes for access governance audit trail
Workspace admin and sensitive data access controlled through PAM with time-bound approval workflows
PAM-approved access routes through private endpoints to prevent internet exposure of regulatory data

blueprints.editableNotes: Customize this stack for your specific regulatory environment (OSFI, Federal Reserve, GDPR, PIPEDA). Define sensitivity labels matching regulatory classifications (e.g., 'OSFI Confidential', 'Trade Secret'). Configure PAM approval workflows for compliance officer reviews.
keyGovernanceRequirements: Implement CMK encryption with regulated key escrow and audit logging
Establish sensitivity classification scheme aligned with regulatory requirements
Deploy SIEM integration with 90-day+ audit log retention for regulatory inspections
Implement PAM with separation of duties: requestor, approver, and executor roles

activities: CMK encryption and key management setup
Network isolation and private link configuration
SIEM integration and audit log export
DLP policy rule creation and testing
Review audit logs and compliance dashboards

blueprints.businessCase: Automates compliance reporting for complex multi-country employment regulations (wage/hour, benefits, leave). Reduces audit preparation time by 80% through continuous compliance monitoring. Tracks regulatory changes and maintains audit trails for government audits, reducing penalties and remediation costs.

blueprints.name: Secure HR Policy Chatbot (GenAI)
blueprints.description: RAG-based conversational AI agent grounded in HR policies and employee data with row-level security enforcement and audit logging. Can be customized as a domain-specific assistant for content generation (policy summaries, FAQs, onboarding guides) or Q&A, enabling both conversational interfaces and automated content creation.

patternFlow.description: Gold-layer HR policies, benefits documentation, and employee data indexed as vectors for RAG retrieval
RAG retriever applies row-level security to ensure employees only see personally relevant information (their benefits, vacation balance)
Secure RAG context fed into Copilot Studio for natural conversational interface with guardrails
Copilot response generation constrained with HR-specific guardrails to prevent hallucinations and inappropriate answers
All chat interactions and guardrail violations logged to SIEM for compliance auditing

blueprints.editableNotes: Customize the chatbot for your HR context by selecting which policies, benefits information, and employee data to ground the RAG retriever in. Define guardrails to prevent the chatbot from answering sensitive questions (e.g., compensation, performance reviews). Configure escalation flows for complex questions. Optionally extend for content generation use cases (policy summaries, FAQ creation, onboarding materials) by adjusting LLM prompts and output templates.
keyGovernanceRequirements: Define RLS rules for chatbot to protect employee personal data (salary, SSN, medical info)
Document guardrails and test for prompt injection/jailbreak attempts before production
Enable audit logging of all chatbot interactions including question, response, and RLS context applied
Establish escalation workflow for guardrail violations and questions requiring human HR review

activities: Vector database and RAG infrastructure setup
Row-level security implementation in RAG layer
HR policy document indexing and vectorization
Select and curate HR policy documents
Define guardrails to prevent sensitive answers
Design escalation workflows and approve conversations

blueprints.businessCase: Reduces HR support ticket volume by 40-60% by automating responses to frequently asked HR questions (benefits, policies, time-off). Provides consistent, compliant information 24/7 without human error risk. Enables HR teams to focus on strategic initiatives while maintaining governance and preventing unauthorized data disclosure.

blueprints.name: Employee Lifecycle Data Pipeline
blueprints.description: Full hire-to-retire data pipeline capturing employee lifecycle events with change data capture, consent management, data retention, and support for data subject requests.
blueprints.audience: HR Operations, Data Governance, Privacy Officers

patternFlow.description: Bronze layer captures all employee lifecycle events (hire, promotion, termination) via change data capture
CDC events processed into Slowly Changing Dimension Type 2 to maintain full employment history with effective dates
SCD records validated for completeness (all required lifecycle fields present, dates consistent)
Valid lifecycle records assigned retention policies based on employee status and contract terms
Retention lifecycle enables data subject request fulfillment by identifying and anonymizing/deleting records per policy
Lifecycle records labeled with consent status and retention period for auditing DSR compliance

blueprints.editableNotes: Customize retention policies for your organization's legal and business requirements (e.g., PIPEDA requires 1-year post-termination retention for severance audits). Define data quality checks matching your HRIS systems (Workday, SuccessFactors). Configure DSR templates for employee data portability requests.
keyGovernanceRequirements: Document retention policy for each employee lifecycle stage and jurisdiction (Canada, US, EU)
Implement data quality validation rules with alerting for missing hire dates, employment terminations
Create DSR fulfillment workflow with legal review before deletion of historical employment data
Maintain audit log of retention policy enforcement and DSR requests with approval chains

activities: Data retention and lifecycle management policies
Lifecycle event schema and transformation design
Map lifecycle events to business processes
Review DSR fulfillment before data deletion

blueprints.businessCase: Reduces onboarding time-to-productivity from 6 months to 3-4 months through systematic data integration across HR systems. Improves offboarding security by automating access revocation and data archival. Creates comprehensive employee lifecycle records for analytics, enabling better retention insights.

blueprints.name: Multi-Country HR Analytics (Data Residency)
blueprints.description: Separate Canadian and US employee data with aggregate-only cross-border reporting to comply with data residency requirements while enabling global insights.
blueprints.audience: Global HR Leadership, Compliance, Data Governance

patternFlow.description: Data residency rules define separate Bronze/Silver/Gold medallion stacks per country (Canada East, US East)
Each country's medallion stack encrypted with region-specific customer-managed keys (Canada uses Canadian key vault)
RLS prevents Canadian users from accessing US employee data and vice versa at semantic model layer
Cross-border aggregate reporting anonymizes individual employee records with k-anonymity before combining countries
Hub workspace in neutral region aggregates anonymized global metrics; spoke workspaces maintain country-specific detailed data

blueprints.editableNotes: Customize data residency rules for your organization's footprint (Canada, US, EU, Asia). Define k-anonymity thresholds per country based on workforce size (e.g., k=5 minimum for any demographic slice). Configure hub-spoke topology matching your governance structure.
keyGovernanceRequirements: Document data flows and residency requirements for each country with legal review
Implement separate encryption keys per country with key rotation per local compliance requirements
Define anonymization thresholds (k-anonymity) for cross-border reporting based on country privacy laws
Establish hub-spoke workspace access controls with country-level RLS at semantic model layer

activities: RLS rule design for country isolation

blueprints.businessCase: Enables multi-country HR analytics while respecting data residency regulations (GDPR, PIPEDA) across regions. Reduces compliance risk through automatic data localization and audit trails. Supports global talent strategy with consistent metrics while maintaining legal and regulatory compliance.

blueprints.name: Governed Self-Service BI Platform
blueprints.description: Enable HR analysts to build reports on certified data without creating shadow analytics by providing governed self-service with certified models, deployment pipelines, and metrics scorecards.
blueprints.audience: HR Analytics Teams, Business Analysts, HR Leadership

patternFlow.description: Gold-layer HR metrics tables certified by data governance team for safe self-service analysis
Certified model deployed to hub workspace as shared analytics foundation; spokes enable team-specific reports
Hub model promoted to production via automated deployment pipeline with testing, version control, and rollback
Deployed models inherit sensitivity labels from source data to enforce security in self-service reports
Workspace permissions aligned with sensitivity labels: internal data visible to HR, confidential data to leadership only
Metrics scorecards built on certified model in governed workspace, providing single source of truth for HR KPIs

blueprints.editableNotes: Customize certified metrics to match your organization's core HR KPIs (headcount, turnover, promotion velocity, diversity). Define governance approval workflow for new metrics (data owner approval, security review). Configure deployment pipeline with testing thresholds for metric validation.
keyGovernanceRequirements: Establish semantic model certification process with data owner sign-off before self-service use
Define deployment pipeline with automated testing, peer review, and change log for all model updates
Configure workspace permissions and sensitivity labels matching organizational security policies
Create metrics scorecard governance to prevent metric proliferation and ensure consistency across org
blueprints.businessCase: Reduces BI development backlog by 50% by empowering business users to create ad-hoc reports within governance guardrails. Eliminates rogue analytics through centralized semantic model and audit logging. Reduces time-to-insight for HR operational questions from days to hours.

blueprints.name: Conversational HR Reporting Analyst
blueprints.description: A Fabric Data Agent-powered conversational interface that lets HR professionals ask natural language questions and receive reliable, data-driven answers. Uses whitelisted queries, curated semantic models, and a query translation layer that maps business questions to the appropriate metrics, datasets, and pre-validated queries.
blueprints.audience: HR Business Partners, People Analytics Consumers, HR Leaders

patternFlow.description: Gold-layer HR metrics tables feed directly into DirectLake semantic model
DirectLake semantic model serves as trusted data foundation for Data Agent queries
Data Agent respects row-level security rules to return only data users are authorized to see
Column-level security masks sensitive fields even within authorized rows
Data Agent response generation constrained with HR-specific guardrails and query whitelisting
All Data Agent queries and responses logged to SIEM for audit and compliance tracking

blueprints.editableNotes: Customize by defining the query translation layer — map common natural language questions (e.g., 'What's our turnover rate?' or 'Show me headcount by department') to specific metrics, semantic model tables, and pre-validated DAX queries. Use few-shot examples in Data Agent instructions (up to 15K chars) to steer the agent toward trusted analytical paths. Define guardrails to prevent cross-boundary queries and sensitive data exploration.
keyGovernanceRequirements: Document whitelisted queries and query translation mappings
Validate example queries in Data Agent instructions with HR stakeholders
Test row-level and column-level security enforcement with sample user queries
Enable audit logging of all Data Agent interactions for compliance tracking

activities: Semantic model design with business-friendly names and measures
Data Agent instruction authoring (up to 15K chars)
Example query curation and few-shot learning setup
Define which questions the agent should answer
Test Data Agent accuracy with sample queries
Acceptance testing and feedback on response quality

blueprints.businessCase: Democratizes HR analytics by enabling natural language queries without SQL knowledge. Reduces time-to-answer for ad-hoc HR questions by 70%. Enables faster decision-making for scenarios like sudden turnover spikes, location-based hiring challenges, or workforce rebalancing needs.

blueprints.name: HR Policy & Domain Knowledge Content Engine
blueprints.description: A customizable, domain-specific AI assistant that aids HR teams in understanding complex policies, generating policy summaries, drafting communications, and creating domain-specific content (onboarding guides, policy explainers, FAQ documents). Combines RAG-grounded policy knowledge with LLM generation capabilities and content guardrails.
blueprints.audience: HR Service Delivery, Total Rewards, Talent Acquisition, HR Communications

patternFlow.description: Gold-layer HR policy documents and reference materials indexed as vectors in RAG knowledge base
RAG retriever grounds Copilot Studio with authoritative policy context
LLM layer generates policy summaries, communications drafts, and content from grounded context
Generated content validated against HR guardrails to ensure tone, brand consistency, and accuracy
Generated content inherits sensitivity labels from source policy documents
Content generation requests and approvals audited in SIEM for compliance

blueprints.editableNotes: Customize by curating HR policy documents, benefits guides, and company procedures to index in the RAG knowledge base. Define content generation templates for common use cases (policy summaries, FAQ responses, onboarding checklists). Configure LLM prompts to match your organization's tone and communication style. Set guardrails for sensitive topics (compensation, legal disclaimers).
keyGovernanceRequirements: Establish policy document curation and versioning process
Document LLM prompt templates and content generation guidelines
Validate generated content accuracy and brand alignment before publishing
Enable audit logging of all content generation requests and approvals

activities: Vector database and RAG infrastructure setup
Access control and sensitivity label configuration
Curate and maintain policy document library
Define guardrails and content tone guidelines

blueprints.businessCase: Ensures HR policies are always current and consistent across the organization. Reduces policy-related compliance violations and legal disputes through version control and audit trails. Enables scalable distribution of approved policies to all employees without manual updates.

blueprints.name: Template-Governed Artifact Generation
blueprints.description: An automated pipeline for creating presentation-ready Word documents and PowerPoint slides that adhere to strict organizational template standards, visual guidelines, and branding requirements. Uses parameterized notebooks to pull data, apply statistical analysis, and generate formatted outputs that are mostly ready for stakeholder consumption.
blueprints.audience: People Analytics Team, HR Leadership Reporting, Executive Briefing Teams

patternFlow.description: Gold-layer HR metrics feed into DirectLake semantic model for parameterized notebook queries
Parameterized Spark notebooks query semantic model for fresh data and run statistical analyses
LLM layer generates executive summaries and narrative sections for presentation slides
Generated artifacts inherit sensitivity labels from source data
Output files and templates stored in Git for version tracking and audit compliance

blueprints.editableNotes: Customize by defining parameterized notebook templates that pull your HR metrics, apply relevant statistical tests, and populate locked Word/PowerPoint templates with branded formatting. Version control all templates in Git to track changes and enforce brand standards. Configure LLM prompts to generate executive narratives matching your organization's voice.
keyGovernanceRequirements: Define and version-control presentation templates with locked formatting sections
Document parameterized notebook logic and statistical methods
Validate generated artifacts for accuracy and brand compliance before distribution
Maintain template change history in Git with approval workflows

activities: Output file storage and lifecycle management
Design presentation templates and brand standards
Define content requirements and narrative tone
Validate statistical accuracy before stakeholder distribution

blueprints.businessCase: Reduces executive reporting time by 60% through automated artifact generation and formatting. Ensures consistent branding and methodology across all reports, improving stakeholder confidence. Enables rapid scenario analysis and what-if planning for HR strategic initiatives.

blueprints.name: Governed Sandbox Analytics Environment
blueprints.description: A flexible, governed workspace where analysts can manually upload files (CSV, Excel, JSON), combine them with production Lakehouse data via OneLake shortcuts, run ad-hoc statistical testing, and generate insights — all within a controlled environment with time-limited data retention and audit trails. Supports both one-off exploratory analysis and recurring parameterized insight generation.
blueprints.audience: People Analytics Team, HR Data Scientists, Business Analysts

patternFlow.description: Sandbox Lakehouse accepts manual file uploads; OneLake shortcuts connect to production Gold-layer data
Shortcuts provide access to production data without copying; sandbox maintains separate Bronze/Silver layers
Analysts run statistical tests (t-tests, regression, ANOVA) in notebooks on combined sandbox and production data
LLM layer interprets statistical test results and generates plain-language insights
Output insights respect row-level security rules for sensitive data
Sandbox artifacts inherit and display sensitivity labels from source data

blueprints.editableNotes: Customize by configuring the sandbox Lakehouse with auto-schema detection for uploaded files. Define OneLake shortcuts to your production Gold tables. Configure retention policies (e.g., 90-day auto-deletion) and audit logging. Create statistical testing notebook templates for common analyses (cohort comparisons, trend analysis, correlation studies).
keyGovernanceRequirements: Establish sandbox Lakehouse with time-bound data retention (e.g., 90 days)
Configure OneLake shortcuts to production data without copying
Enable audit logging of all uploads, analysis, and exports
Require approval workflows for exports and sensitive data uploads

activities: OneLake shortcut setup to production data
Audit logging and access control configuration
Data quality checks on uploaded files
Upload source files and validate data quality
Perform statistical tests and interpret results
Approve insights before distribution to stakeholders

blueprints.businessCase: Enables fast experimentation with new analytics and ML models without impacting production systems. Provides data scientists a safe space to test advanced analyses before deployment. Reduces time-to-value for new analytics initiatives while maintaining production stability and governance.

metadata.description: Pre-built use case blueprints showing recommended pattern stacks for common People Analytics scenarios
